ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Machine learning algorithm used (e.g., Linear Regression, Random Forest) */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for training */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the model */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the model (e.g., training, deployed, archived) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the model was last updated */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who owns the model */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., CSV file, database) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was created */,
  "dataset_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the dataset */,
  "data_size" TEXT /* Size of the dataset in bytes */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the dataset (e.g., CSV, JSON, Parquet) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the dataset was last updated */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who owns the dataset */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "iterations" (
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each iteration */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being trained */,
  "iteration_number" INTEGER /* Iteration number (e.g., 0, 1, 2, etc.) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the iteration */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the iteration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the iteration (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* JSON or text field to store hyperparameters used in this iteration */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the iteration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("iteration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_iterations_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the iteration the metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., MAE, Lq norm) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the metric */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_metrics_iteration_id FOREIGN KEY ("iteration_id") REFERENCES iterations ("iteration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_rates" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error rate */,
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the iteration the error rate belongs to */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value (e.g., 0.1, 0.01, 0.5) */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate for the given threshold */,
  "error_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the error rate */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the error rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_rates_iteration_id FOREIGN KEY ("iteration_id") REFERENCES iterations ("iteration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithm_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Machine learning algorithm the parameter belongs to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter */,
  "parameter_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the parameter */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the model or dataset */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being accessed */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the model version (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version the metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., MAE, Lq norm) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the metric */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_metrics_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES model_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_error_rates" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error rate */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version the error rate belongs to */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value (e.g., 0.1, 0.01, 0.5) */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate for the given threshold */,
  "error_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the error rate */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the error rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_error_rates_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES model_versions ("version_id")
)
